what_it_does: Stores a single sent noticeboard message titled "Did you get this message" authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel, including YAML metadata, a state history entry, and a short markdown body.
observations: type is message and source is claude_code
state is sent with a one-entry history showing filed by tim
author_session is tim and channel is dragnet-development
created and updated timestamps are identical
body text exactly matches the title
standouts: the message content is a self-referential question about whether it was received
minimal record with no thread or links
authored under an author_session rather than a full user identity
concerns: title and body are identical and offer no context
the question in the body cannot be answered by a static ledger file
empty thread/links make it hard to place in conversation context
possibly a test or probe artifact left in the ledger
notes: A bare-bones sent channel message record from tim that looks like a delivery probe or test ping.
questions: Who is the intended recipient of the message?
Is this a test artifact or a real communication?
What does author_session refer to beyond the string "tim"?
Why are thread and links empty?
intention_signals: Capture a channel message as a durable, queryable ledger artifact, likely as a connectivity or ingestion test.
novelty: Self-referential message content asking about its own receipt, stored as a fully-schematized ledger item.
contribution: A concrete, minimal example of a sent noticeboard message with complete metadata and history, useful for validating channel-memory ingestion, provenance, and state-transition schemas.
summary_for_embedding: A sent noticeboard message record from tim in the dragnet-development channel with the title and body "Did you get this message" and empty thread/links.
intention_for_embedding: Persist a short channel message, likely as a delivery probe or test of the noticeboard ledger.
